items.title: JavaFX Stage Events
items.summary: The JavaFX Stage class represents a window in a JavaFX app. The JavaFX Stage tutorial has been updated with a section about which events you can listen for on a JavaFX Stage (close requested, showing, shown, hiding, hidden).

items.title: Java ThreadLocal
items.summary: A Java ThreadLocal is a class that can store one value internally per thread accessing it. I have updated the JavaThreadLocal tutorial, with a video version among other things.

items.title: Java 15 Features
items.summary: I have updated my Java tutorial intro to list the new features in Java 15.

items.title: JavaFX Node
items.summary: The JavaFX Node class is the base class (superclass) of all nodes (objects / components) added to the JavaFX scene graph. In this JavaFX Node tutorial I explain the basic properties of the JavaFX Node class.

items.title: Java volatile tutorial
items.summary: I have updated the Java volatile tutorial - with a video version among other things.

items.title: JavaFX Intro Update
items.summary: JavaFX is an advanced, hardware accelerated GUI toolkit for Java with new releases every 6 months. I have updated my introduction to JavaFX to be up-to-date.

items.title: Java AtomicLong
items.summary: The Java AtomicLong is a concurrency utility that provides atomic operations on an internal long variable. I have updated this Java AtomicLong tutorial with a few details.

items.title: Java Synchronized
items.summary: The Java synchronized tutorial has been updated - with a video version of the tutorial among other things.

items.title: Java Happens Before Guarantee
items.summary: The Java happens before guarantee provides some restrictions on allowed instruction reordering for the Java VM and CPU. This Java happens before guarantee tutorial explains what these restrictions are. The Java happens before guarantee is actually part of the Java memory model, by the way.

items.title: Java Memory Model
items.summary: The Java Memory Model specifies how the Java VM and threads access memory, and where local variables and objects are stored. I have updated this tutorial - among other things with a video version.

items.title: Java Modules
items.summary: I have updated my Java Modules tutorial about the Java Platform Module System.

items.title: Build Java modules with Ant
items.summary: This tutorial shows how to build Java modules (Java Platform Module System modules) with Ant.

items.title: Java Threads - Create, start and stop threads in Java
items.summary: The tutorial about creating and starting threads in Java has just been updated to be a bit more precise, including a new recording of the video for the topic.

items.title: Java Concurrency and Multithreading
items.summary: The Java Concurrency and multithreading introduction has been updated, among other things with links to video versions of some of the concurrency tutorials - one of which is an introduction to concurrency and multithreading.

items.title: Jackson JsonNode
items.summary: The Jackson JsonNode represents a node in a JSON object graph. I have updated the Jackson JsonNode tutorial to explain how to iterate all fields or field names of a JsonNode.

items.title: Java Iterable
items.summary: I have updated my Java Iterable tutorial - among other things with an explanation of the forEach() method which is an alternative way to iterate the elements of a Java Iterable.

items.title: RION Ops RionObjectWriter
items.summary: The RionObjectWriter can serialize Java objects into RION - our fast, compact, versatile binary data format. The RionObjectWriter tutorial now explains how to write both acyclic and cyclic object graphs into RION.

items.title: Java Iterator
items.summary: The Java Iterator tutorial has been updated to cover everything you need to know about the Iterator interface. Did you know about the Iterator forEachRemaining() method?

items.title: Java Stack Tutorial
items.summary: The Java Stack class is a class stack implementation like you know them from an algorithms and data structures class. The Java Stack tutorial has received another round of updates, so it is now more precise.

items.title: RION Ops RionObjectReader
items.summary: The RionObjectReader can read RION Object fields representing single objects or acyclic and/or cyclic object graphs.

items.title: Java Record
items.summary: A Java Record is a class representing an immutable data record using a concise syntax. Java Records is a new feature in Java 14. This Java Record tutorial explains how records work.

items.title: Java Collections
items.summary: I have updated my Java Collections introduction, with among other things a link to my Java Collections tutorial video playlist.

items.title: RION Ops - RionReader
items.summary: The RION Ops for Java RionReader component enables you to read RION data fields reasonably easily, and with high performance. The RionReader tutorial now explains how to traverse a RION Table field.

items.title: RION Ops - RionWriter
items.summary: The RION Ops for Java RionWriter component enables you to write RION data fields reasonably easily, and with high performance. Now this tutorial also explains how to write RION Table fields.

items.title: RION Ops - Introduction
items.summary: Following release 0.11.0 of RION Ops, the Java toolkit for working with our compact, fast, versatile data format RION, I have updated the introduction to the RION Ops toolkit. More updates coming!

items.title: Java Deque
items.summary: The Java Deque interface represents a double ended queue. The Java Deque tutorial has received a major update, including a video version of the tutorial.

items.title: Java 13 String Methods
items.summary: The Java String tutorial has been updated to explain the new preview methods added in Java 13.

items.title: JavaFX GridPane
items.summary: The JavaFX GridPane is a layout container that lays out its children in a grid. The JavaFX GridPane tutorial has been updated.

items.title: JavaFX Effects
items.summary: JavaFX effects are graphical effects you can apply to a JavaFX Node - for instance blur, drop shadow, reflection etc. This JavaFX Effects introduction guides you through the basics of how to apply effects in your JavaFX applications.

items.title: Java Queue Tutorial Updated
items.summary: The Java Queue tutorial has received a major update, including a video version of the tutorial too.

items.title: HTML5 File API
items.summary: The HTML5 File API tutorial has been updated with information about how to upload a file from the browser via the new fetch() AJAX function in JavaScript.

items.title: JavaFX Text
items.summary: The JavaFX Text control is used to display text inside your JavaFX application. This JavaFX tutorial explains the basics of the JavaFX Text control.

items.title: JavaFX Color
items.summary: The JavaFX Color class and related classes are used to set e.g. the background color, border color etc of JavaFX controls. This JavaFX Color tutorial explains how to create JavaFX Color instances using various different color codes (web, RGB, HSB).

items.title: Maven Commands
items.summary: Maven Commands tutorial updated - to include an example of how to skip unit tests during build (sometimes useful).

items.title: Java Map Video Tutorial
items.summary: I have published a new video tutorial about the Java Map interface.

items.title: SVG Tutorial
items.summary: I have updated the SVG tutorial introduction, and among other things made it more mobile friendly.

items.title: JavaFX Concurrency
items.summary: This JavaFX Concurrency tutorial introduces how threads and concurrency works in JavaFX.

items.title: Java Concurrency
items.summary: Java Concurrency introduction has been updated.

items.title: Java Concurrency Utils
items.summary: Java Concurrency Utils introduction has been updated.

items.title: Java Set Tutorial Video
items.summary: I have created a video version of my Java Set tutorial - for anyone who prefers video over text.

items.title: Java instanceof
items.summary: The Java instanceof operator is used to determine if a given Java object is an instance of a specific class or interface. In Java 14 the Java instanceof operator has been extended with new, pattern matching functionality that makes it more concise to use when downcasting after an instanceof check.

items.title: Java Synchronized
items.summary: The Java synchronized tutorial has been updated to include a lot more details about how synchronized blocks in Java works.

items.title: Java Map
items.summary: The Java Map tutorial has been updated to promote the use of generic types (Java Generics) with maps.

items.title: Java Set
items.summary: The Java Set tutorial has been updated to promote the use of generic types (Java Generics) with sets.

items.title: Java List
items.summary: The Java List tutorial has been updated to promote the use of generic types (Java Generics) with lists.

items.title: Java Text Blocks
items.summary: Java Text Blocks is a new feature in Java 13. I have updated the Java String tutorial to explain how the new Java text block feature works. A Java text block is also called a multi line String.

items.title: Your First Java App
items.summary: I have updated the tutorial about how to create your first Java app to use IntelliJ IDEA Community Edition 2019.3.3 and Java 13.

items.title: Installing the Java SDK
items.summary: I have updated the tutorial about how to install the Java SDK to match the reality from Java 13 and forward.

items.title: What is Java?
items.summary: I have updated the What is Java tutorial to reflect what Java is as of Java 13 and forward.

items.title: Java AtomicStampedReference
items.summary: I have updated the Java AtomicStampedRefence tutorial somewhat. The AtomicStampedReference can be used to avoid the classic A-B-A concurrency problem.

items.title: Java switch Expression Improvements in Java 13
items.summary: I have updated the Java switch tutorial to explain the Java switch expression improvements in Java 13.

items.title: Maven Java Compiler Version
items.summary: This tutorial explains how to set the Java compiler version to compile your project with, inside the POM file for the project. The tutorial now explains how to enable the preview features in Java 12 and 13.

items.title: CSS display
items.summary: The CSS display property specifies how an HTML element is to be rendered (displayed) in the HTML element flow of an HTML page. The CSS display tutorial has been updated to cover more valid property values.

items.title: Java Threads
items.summary: Java Threads are like virtual CPUs that can execute parts of your application in parallel. I have updated the tutorial about how to create and start threads in Java.

items.title: JavaFX HBox
items.summary: The JavaFX HBox control is a layout control which lays out its child nodes next to each other on a horizontal row. The JavaFX HBox tutorial has been updated with information about child node margins plus the hgrow and fillHeight properties.

items.title: Java Map
items.summary: The Java Map tutorial has been corrected to say that null keys are allowed in a Java Map. Counter-intuitive - but true.

items.title: JavaFX VBox
items.summary: The JavaFX VBox can layout its child components in a vertical column - on top of each other. The JavaFX VBox tutorial has been updated to explain concepts like fillWidth, CSS styling, margins around child nodes etc.

items.title: Java Resizable Array
items.summary: I have updated the Java Resizable Array tutorial to explain why a Java ArrayList cannot always do the job (performance reasons).

items.title: RION Ops RionObjectWriter
items.summary: The RionObjectReader makes it easy to write Java objects out to RION. To serialize Java objects as RION, in other words.

items.title: RION Ops RionObjectReader
items.summary: The RionObjectReader makes it easy to read RION data into Java objects. To deserialize Java objects from RION, in other words.
